text.title: A11y Audit per-Screen Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: 10 testable rules grounding the A11y Audit per-Screen Checklist methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the last rule binds the decision tree skip path.

rule.statement: Each screen MUST expose at least one of `<main>`, `<nav>`, `<header>`, `<footer>` landmarks. Pages with only divs are rejected.
rule.rationale: WCAG 1.3.1 + screen-reader navigation requires landmark structure.

rule.statement: Tab order MUST follow visual order top-to-bottom, left-to-right. Reversed or random tab order is rejected.
rule.rationale: broken focus order is a P1 in audits.

rule.statement: All text MUST meet ≥4.5:1 contrast (≥3:1 for large text). Exceptions only for inactive/disabled elements, which must be marked.
Every meaningful image MUST carry alt text; decorative images MUST carry alt="". `alt="image"`, `alt="icon"`, or no alt at all are rejected.
Every interactive element MUST be reachable + activatable via keyboard alone. Mouse-only elements are rejected.
ARIA roles + names MUST match the underlying semantics. `role="button"` on a div without `tabindex` is rejected; native `<button>` is preferred.
Form errors MUST be programmatically associated with the input (aria-describedby OR proximate text). Toast-only errors are rejected.
Animations / parallax / autoplay MUST respect `prefers-reduced-motion`. Always-on motion is rejected.
Icon-only buttons MUST carry an accessible name (aria-label or visually-hidden text). Bare icon buttons are rejected.
rule.rationale: screen readers cannot infer name from glyph.

rule.statement: When the decision tree routes to `skip-this-methodology`, the agent MUST emit a structured `skip` decision (with reason + suggested-sibling) rather than running A11y Audit per-Screen Checklist under degraded conditions.
rule.rationale: decision-tree contract. The decision tree is the only place that knows the routing; skipping must be observable so the orchestrator can log the routed-elsewhere path.
